message:
docs: update architecture anchors and convergence observations

architecture.md:
- Mentions NativeOutputCoalescer in watcher startup section
- Adds search/* to module listing
- Notes native mux/server work split into dedicated crates

ft-3681t-convergence-architecture.md:
- Four new traceability rows: A-CLI-002 (robot API contracts inventory),
  A-OBS-003 (burst coalescing), A-STO-003 (hybrid search),
  A-MUX-001 (native mux split)
- Corresponding prose in implementation observations section documenting
  already-existing code to close traceability gaps

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -76,6 +76,8 @@ Watcher startup currently wires these components in-process:
 - capture relay and queueing
 - persistence + detection (`spawn_persistence_task`)
 - maintenance/retention and snapshot triggers.
+- burst protection on native output via `NativeOutputCoalescer` before segments
+  hit storage/pattern scanning.
 
 Core passive loop contract: observe/store/detect only. Side effects are delegated to workflow/policy layers.
 
@@ -84,6 +86,8 @@ Core passive loop contract: observe/store/detect only. Side effects are delegate
 - `wezterm.rs`: backend adapter trait (`WeztermInterface`) and concrete handle construction.
 - `ingest.rs`: pane discovery, fingerprinting, delta extraction, gap generation, ingest telemetry.
 - `storage.rs`: SQLite schema, migrations, WAL mode, FTS5 index/triggers, writer queue, query APIs.
+- `search/*`: lexical, semantic, and hybrid retrieval services layered on the
+  same storage substrate.
 - `patterns.rs`: rule packs, anchor/regex matching, telemetry, detection objects.
 - `events.rs`: bounded broadcast fanout and typed event stream (`PatternDetected`, `GapDetected`, workflow lifecycle, user-var events).
 - `workflows/`: durable workflow trait/execution engine/runner/locks and step orchestration.
@@ -105,6 +109,9 @@ This schema is the contract behind status/search/events/workflow/audit CLI and r
 ### Robot mode + policy seam
 
 - Robot command handling lives in `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` under `RobotCommands`.
+- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_api_contracts.rs` is the clearest machine
+  contract inventory for the current robot surface (search/events/workflow/rules/
+  reservations/mission/tx families).
 - Robot read paths (`state`, `get-text`, `search`, `events`) use WezTerm/storage + policy checks.
 - Robot/action paths (`send`, workflow run, approvals) are routed through policy-gated injectors and workflow runners.
 - MCP mirrors this model through feature-gated core modules (`mcp*` in `frankenterm-core` + `Commands::Mcp` in CLI).
@@ -113,6 +120,9 @@ This schema is the contract behind status/search/events/workflow/audit CLI and r
 
 - Optional surfaces are enabled via crate features (`mcp`, `web`, `distributed`, `metrics`, `tui`, `ftui`, `sync`, `semantic-search`, `native-wezterm`, etc.).
 - Current operational backend remains WezTerm compatibility bridge (`wezterm.rs` + vendored integrations), while native/runtime expansion continues in `frankenterm-core`.
+- Headless mux/server work is already split into `crates/frankenterm-mux-server`
+  and `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl`, which means native mux/server
+  evolution is a validation/productization problem more than a bootstrap-one.
 
 ## Deterministic state (OSC 133)

@@ -319,8 +319,10 @@ contract so traceability checks can be performed mechanically.
 | Anchor ID | Architecture Contract | Code Anchor(s) | Probe |
 |---|---|---|---|
 | `A-CLI-001` | CLI dispatch routes `watch` and `robot` into stable runtime entry points | `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs::run`, `run_watcher_with_backoff`, `run_watcher` | `rg -n "async fn run\\(|run_watcher_with_backoff|run_watcher" crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` |
+| `A-CLI-002` | Robot control-plane inventory already covers search, events, workflow, rules, reservation, mission, and tx-oriented machine contracts | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_api_contracts.rs::RobotApiContract`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_types.rs` transactional/result envelopes | `rg -n "SearchExplain|WorkflowRun|Reserve|MissionState|TxPlan|Transaction" crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_api_contracts.rs crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_types.rs` |
 | `A-OBS-001` | Observation path is passive-first: discover -> capture -> persist -> detect -> publish | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs::ObservationRuntime::start`, `spawn_discovery_task`, `spawn_capture_task`, `spawn_persistence_task` | `rg -n "ObservationRuntime|spawn_discovery_task|spawn_capture_task|spawn_persistence_task" crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs` |
 | `A-OBS-002` | Detection events are persisted, then published for workflow fanout | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs::spawn_persistence_task` (`record_event` followed by `Event::PatternDetected` publish in the persistence path) | `rg -n "record_event\\(|Event::PatternDetected" crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs` |
+| `A-OBS-003` | Burst native output is coalesced before persistence to keep the passive pipeline deterministic under load | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs::NativeOutputCoalescer`, `drain_due`, `flush_pane` | `rg -n "NativeOutputCoalescer|drain_due|flush_pane" crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs` |
 | `A-PAT-001` | Pattern engine uses anchor-first candidate generation with optional regex extraction and context dedupe | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs::PatternEngine::detect`, `detect_with_context` | `rg -n "pub fn detect\\(|detect_with_context\\(" crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` |
 | `A-EVT-001` | Event bus supports typed fanout for deltas/detections/signals | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs::EventBus::publish`, `subscribe_detections`, `subscribe_deltas`, `subscribe_signals` | `rg -n "pub fn publish\\(|subscribe_detections|subscribe_deltas|subscribe_signals" crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs` |
 | `A-WF-001` | Workflow runtime is event-driven from detection bus and lock-mediated | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs::WorkflowRunner::run`, `handle_detection`, `run_workflow` | `rg -n "pub async fn run\\(|handle_detection\\(|run_workflow\\(" crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs` |
@@ -329,8 +331,10 @@ contract so traceability checks can be performed mechanically.
 | `A-POL-002` | Mutation execution path is policy-gated and auditable | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs::PolicyGatedInjector::send_text`, `inject` | `rg -n "pub async fn send_text\\(|async fn inject\\(" crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs` |
 | `A-STO-001` | Storage substrate is SQLite+WAL with bounded async writer and read-side search | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs::StorageHandle::with_config`, `record_event`, `search_with_results` | `rg -n "journal_mode = WAL|with_config\\(|record_event\\(|search_with_results\\(" crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs` |
 | `A-STO-002` | Policy/MCP actions are persisted through redacted audit records | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs::record_audit_action_redacted` | `rg -n "record_audit_action_redacted" crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs` |
+| `A-STO-003` | Search substrate already spans lexical, semantic, and hybrid retrieval modes on top of the same persistence seam | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs::search_with_results`, `search_fts_with_snippets`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/*` | `rg -n "search_with_results|search_fts_with_snippets|SearchMode|HybridSearchService" crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs crates/frankenterm-core/src/search` |
 | `A-SWM-001` | Swarm launch planning already models weighted slots, phase ordering, and invariant checks | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_profiles.rs::FleetLaunchPlan`, `slots_for_phase`, `program_weight`, `invariant_violations` | `rg -n "FleetLaunchPlan|slots_for_phase|program_weight|invariant_violations" crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_profiles.rs` |
 | `A-SWM-002` | Work-claim/deconfliction kernels exist for beads import, conflict detection, and Agent Mail signaling | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/swarm_work_queue.rs::sync_to_queue`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs::detect_conflicts_with_resources`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_agent_mail.rs::emit_conflict_signals_at` | `rg -n "sync_to_queue|detect_conflicts_with_resources|emit_conflict_signals_at" crates/frankenterm-core/src/swarm_work_queue.rs crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_agent_mail.rs` |
+| `A-MUX-001` | Native mux/server work is already split into a thin headless binary and shared server-domain registration layer, independent of GUI rendering | `crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/main.rs::spawn_listener`, `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/lib.rs::update_mux_domains_for_server`, `local::LocalListener::with_domain` | `rg -n "spawn_listener|update_mux_domains_for_server|LocalListener::with_domain" crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/main.rs crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/lib.rs` |
 | `A-TEL-001` | Observability plane already normalizes cross-plane telemetry into a unified record model | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_telemetry.rs::UnifiedTelemetryRecord`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_health.rs::with_telemetry_log`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy_metrics.rs` | `rg -n "UnifiedTelemetryRecord|with_telemetry_log|PolicyMetricsDashboard" crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_telemetry.rs crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_health.rs crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy_metrics.rs` |
 | `A-CON-001` | Connector fabric bridge primitives already route inbound signals and normalize outbound actions | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_inbound_bridge.rs::ConnectorInboundBridge::route_signal`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs::ConnectorOutboundBridge::process_event`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_event_model.rs` | `rg -n "route_signal|process_event|ConnectorInboundBridge|ConnectorOutboundBridge" crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_inbound_bridge.rs crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_event_model.rs` |
 | `A-MCP-001` | MCP server exposes robot-parity tools/resources and wraps storage-affecting tools with audit middleware | `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_bridge.rs::build_server_with_db` (`AuditedToolHandler` registrations) | `rg -n "build_server_with_db|AuditedToolHandler" crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_bridge.rs` |
@@ -342,6 +346,19 @@ contract so traceability checks can be performed mechanically.
   (`wezterm_handle_from_config` wiring in watcher and robot handlers).
 - The passive observation contract is concretely implemented in the runtime
   persistence task; mutation surfaces are separated via policy-gated injectors.
+- Headless mux/server work is no longer hypothetical: the workspace already has
+  a dedicated `crates/frankenterm-mux-server` binary and
+  `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl` library, so downstream native mux/server
+  work should treat validation and operator integration as the main gap rather
+  than greenfield server bootstrapping.
+- Search is already more than plain FTS parity: current storage/search code
+  exposes lexical, semantic, and hybrid retrieval modes, which means most
+  remaining `ft-3681t.4.*` search work is about contract hardening and surface
+  alignment rather than inventing a new retrieval substrate.
+- Runtime ingestion already contains burst-control primitives such as native
+  output coalescing and vendored streaming fallback paths, which should be
+  reflected in any observability or scale/degradation design work instead of
+  assuming a purely polling-based watcher.
 - Swarm launch planning, work-claim deconfliction, unified telemetry rollups,
   and connector bridge primitives already exist in code as partial convergence
   surfaces; the remaining gap is productizing them behind stable public
